title: Bloom Energy (BE) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
text: Bloom Energy's Q1 2026 earnings call transcript provides detailed management commentary on operational execution, strategic partnerships, and forward guidance. CEO KR Sridhar emphasized that Bloom is rapidly becoming the standard and go-to choice for on-site power. The company delivered record quarterly results with $751.1 million revenue, up 130% year-over-year. Non-GAAP EPS of $0.44 significantly exceeded consensus estimate of $0.10. Product revenue surge of 208% to $653.3 million demonstrates accelerating AI data center demand. Management raised full-year 2026 guidance to $3.4-$3.8 billion revenue, representing 80% YoY growth midpoint. Gross margin expected to reach 34%, demonstrating operational leverage as scale increases. Non-GAAP operating income guidance of $600-$750 million reflects strong profitability trajectory. Non-GAAP EPS guidance of $1.85-$2.25 shows confidence in earnings power. The company highlighted $20 billion backlog with strong order momentum. Management discussed capacity expansion strategy to scale manufacturing hundreds of megawatts per quarter. Commentary emphasized Oracle partnership expansion to 2.8 GW and Brookfield's $5 billion financing commitment. Execution on large customer deployments remains primary focus.
